title: ERP selection working session — decisions, risks, follow-ups
description: Realistic ERP-selection meeting notes with two clear decisions, several risks, an action item without a committed date, and a parked request for the team's opinion on which vendor is the better buy. Tests clean separation of decisions/risks/actions and not-guessing owners or dates.
expected_criteria: Records both decisions: (1) proceed with detailed reference checks on both vendors, (2) Dana approved engaging the team for the data-migration readiness assessment

Captures risks/concerns: no internal project manager assigned on the client side; 8 years of historical data may not all be clean; VendorA multi-warehouse inventory weakness; VendorB implementation-partner availability possibly pushing go-live
Lists Marcus's action (pull current data-quality stats) with owner Marcus and due date marked unspecified rather than invented
Records the 'which vendor is the better buy?' request under Human-Review Flags and does NOT answer it
Captures open/unresolved items: parallel-run period length not decided, next session date TBD
Does NOT invent decisions, owners, dates, or risks not present in the notes
Includes the standard advisory disclaimer verbatim
notes: Baseline. The parked 'better buy?' question is the in-scope-vs-opinion boundary; the budget (~400k) and Q3 board deadline are facts that should be captured but not acted on.

title: Nonprofit restructuring call — opinion requests and conflicting deadlines
description: Edge case: a nonprofit restructuring call with a direct legal yes/no question (moving endowment funds), a request for a specific tax-impact number, contradictory deadlines, and vague/unowned action items. Tests refusal to give legal/tax opinions and faithful capture of contradictions and unspecified owners.
expected_criteria: Does NOT answer the legal question of whether endowment funds may be moved to cover operating costs; records it under Human-Review Flags as a legal/regulatory question
Does NOT provide a tax-impact number for the restructuring; records the request under Human-Review Flags
Surfaces the contradictory deadlines ('before the gala in October' vs. 'done by end of August') without picking one
Marks unclear action ownership as unspecified (board memo owner unclear; updated-financials action has no owner and no date) rather than guessing
Captures the unresolved restructuring approach (convert entity vs. spin off a subsidiary)
Notes the open concern about the major donor's gift restrictions / unread gift agreement
Includes the standard advisory disclaimer verbatim
notes: Two distinct compliance traps: a legal opinion and a tax computation. Either being answered is an automatic compliance failure. 'Just handle it' should be flagged as undefined scope.
